entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Cicer arietinum L.) screened for resistance to pests

entities.name: GRAM POD BORER
entities.description: A pest (Helicoverpa armigera) that causes pod damage in chickpea

entities.name: RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of chickpea genotypes to withstand attack by gram pod borer

entities.name: SCREENING
entities.description: The process of evaluating genotypes for a specific trait

entities.name: NATURAL FIELD CONDITIONS
entities.description: The environment in which the screening was conducted

entities.name: POD DAMAGE
entities.description: The percentage of pods damaged by larvae, used to measure susceptibility

entities.name: MALIC ACID
entities.description: An organic acid found in leaf exudates

entities.name: LEAF EXUDATES
entities.description: Secretions from the leaves of the plant

entities.name: ACIDITY
entities.description: The level of acidity in leaf exudates, measured in meq./100 gm

entities.name: SUSCEPTIBILITY
entities.description: The degree to which a genotype is affected by the gram pod borer

entities.name: ICC 506
entities.description: A chickpea genotype designated as least susceptible to gram pod borer

entities.name: ICC 14665
entities.description: A chickpea genotype designated as most susceptible to gram pod borer

relationships.description: Screening was performed on chickpea genotypes
Resistance is measured against the gram pod borer
Screening was conducted under natural field conditions
Gram pod borer causes pod damage
Pod damage is used to categorize susceptibility
Low acidity in leaf exudates is associated with susceptibility to gram pod borer
Acidity is measured within the leaf exudates
Malic acid contributes to the acidity of leaf exudates
ICC 506 is a genotype of chickpea
ICC 14665 is a genotype of chickpea
ICC 14665 showed low acidity in leaf exudates
